scope: Arbitrum (42161) Bridge2 0x2df1c51e09aecf9cacb7bc98cb1742757f163df7 (80 tx) + native USDC 0xaf88d065e77c8cc2239327c5edb3a432268e5831 (80 tx window, +500 sampled across pages). HyperEVM (999) CoreWriter/WHYPE/system attempted via Etherscan v2 chainid=999 (rejected). 12 HyperliquidTransaction:* typed-data actions are off-chain (no Etherscan source).
command: set -a; source crates/integration-tests/.env; set +a; curl 'https://api.etherscan.io/v2/api?chainid=42161&module=account&action=txlist&address=<ADDR>&page=1&offset=80&sort=desc&apikey=$ETHERSCAN_API_KEY'; ./target/debug/v3-harness import-etherscan <pull> --chain 42161 --out <corpus>; ./target/debug/v3-harness corpus --root crates/integration-tests/data/golden/v3-decode

coverage_matrix._legend: covered = callkey/typed-data present in registryV2/index; decode = v3-harness corpus probe result; sender_class = end-user wallet vs node/validator infra (from tx.from analysis of the 80-tx window)

pullable_arbitrum_42161.decode: expected ok=true domain=token (ERC20 standard mappers); not separately committed to corpus (standard ERC20 already exercised by uniswap/edge-case corpora)

hyperevm_999_blocked.function: sendRawAction(bytes) — tagged_dispatch v0x01, action_id uint24 BE at data[1:4], 15 actions + default
hyperevm_999_blocked.decode: synthetic fuzz only (tagged_dispatch strategy); 14/15 actions + default map to honest Unknown, action 11 cancel_order_by_cloid maps to perp domain

off_chain_typed_data.source: hand-corpus needed — EIP-712 off-chain signatures have NO Etherscan/on-chain source by nature
off_chain_typed_data.note: Index has 12 typed-data primary types per chain (correction to the task's '12 REST' list — TokenDelegate/ConvertToMultiSigUser/SendMultiSig/UserDexAbstraction/UserSetAbstraction are present in index, while the task brief named CWithdraw/CDeposit which are NOT in index).

notes.detail: manifest rest/withdraw@1.0.0.json maps to typed-data HyperliquidTransaction:Withdraw (action tag 0x00000003) — it is the L1 USDC withdraw signed action, NOT a WHYPE withdraw. Confirmed: rest/withdraw tag=0x00000003 chains=[42161,421614]; whype/withdraw selector=0x2e1a7d4d chains=[999]. Two distinct correct manifests; no drift.

batchedDepositWithPermit decodes to top-level domain=multicall (not token) because the batch fn is split into atomic deposit intents. Corpus expect_domain set to multicall accordingly (probe-confirmed).

summary: HyperLiquid testability is genuinely thin and now cleanly classified into 3 tiers. PULLABLE (Arbitrum 42161): only Bridge2 + native USDC are Etherscan-reachable. Bridge2 has 3 selectors in the 80-tx window — 0xb30b5bce batchedDepositWithPermit (x6, end-user, COVERED, decodes ok with top-level domain=multicall, all 6 committed to corpus) and two infra selectors 0x1f12171f (x37) + 0xc5bdf3ca batchedFinalizeWithdrawals (x37), each driven by a small rotating set of validator/finalizer EOAs (4 distinct senders each) = node infrastructure, correctly OUT of pre-sign wallet scope. Native USDC approve/transfer/transferFrom/permit are all COVERED via existing 42161 callkeys (tokens/42161 dir exists). HYPEREVM-999-BLOCKED: chainid=999 returns Etherscan v2 NOTOK; hyperevmscan.io needs its own api.hyperevmscan.io key (anonymous = NOTOK); HyperEVM RPC (rpc.hyperliquid.xyz/evm, chainId 0x3e7) is live but is not a txlist source. So CoreWriter sendRawAction (15-action tagged_dispatch), WHYPE (5 selectors), and system native-transfer are synthetic-fuzz-only — no real-tx validation possible. OFF-CHAIN-TYPED-DATA: 12 HyperliquidTransaction:* EIP-712 actions per chain (42161/421614) have no on-chain/Etherscan source by nature; hand-corpus of real captured signatures required for positive validation. Net: 0 decoder bugs found; the only 'gaps' are intentionally-out-of-scope infra selectors and the inherent off-chain/HyperEVM testability blocks.
